methods.description: Synthesis of N-deficient g-C3N4/diatomite (NGD) through a thermal oxidation method.

steps.description: 5.0 g of diatomite (1.0 g) and dicyandiamide (4.0 g) were mixed uniformly with 1.5 g of (NH4)2S2O8 in an agate mortar.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix diatomite, dicyandiamide, and ammonium persulfate in an agate mortar

steps.description: The product was then heated at 550 °C for 4 h under an air atmosphere with a heating rate of 2.3 °C/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat mixture at 550 °C for 4 hours under air atmosphere

methods.description: Synthesis of FNGD composites by dissolving NGD and iron acetate in ethanol, followed by sonication, drying, and heating.

steps.description: 0.5 g of NGD and x mmol of C4H6FeO4·4H2O (x = 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 1.2 and 1.8) were dissolved into 5 mL of ethanol and then the suspension was sonicated for 2 h to be evenly dispersed.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve NGD and iron acetate in ethanol and sonicate for 2 hours

steps.description: The suspension was transferred to a vacuum oven and dried at 70 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry suspension in vacuum oven at 70 °C

steps.description: Finally, the powder was heated at 500 °C for 2 h under a nitrogen atmosphere with a heating rate of 5 °C/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat powder at 500 °C for 2 hours under nitrogen atmosphere

methods.description: Characterization techniques used to analyze the phase structure, interaction, morphology, elemental distribution, pore structure, surface chemical bonds, optical adsorption, and electrochemical properties of the prepared materials.

steps.description: X-ray powder diffraction (XRD) was applied to investigate the phase structure of the prepared materials.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform XRD to analyze phase structure

steps.description: Fourier transformed infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy was performed to analyze the interaction of prepared materials.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform FTIR to analyze material interactions

steps.description: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) were employed to observe the morphologies and elemental distribution of the prepared FNGD materials, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Use SEM and EDS to observe morphology and elemental distribution

steps.description: The pore structure parameters of the as-prepared samples were obtained using multipoint N2 adsorption/desorption isotherms with Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) and Barrett-Joyner-Halenda (BJH) methods.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pore structure using BET and BJH methods

steps.description: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was applied to identify the surface chemical bonds of the 0.6-FNGD composite.
steps.searchable_summary: Use XPS to identify surface chemical bonds

steps.description: Ultraviolet-visible diffuse reflectance spectroscopy (UV-vis DRS) was applied to observe the optical adsorption and band structure of the FNGD and contrastive samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform UV-vis DRS to observe optical adsorption and band structure

steps.description: The electrochemical properties, including the electrochemical impedance spectroscopy and transient photocurrent response, of selected samples were tested.
steps.searchable_summary: Test electrochemical properties using impedance spectroscopy and photocurrent response

steps.description: Inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry (ICP-OES) was used to detect the Fe leaching concentration and Fe ion content of 0.6-FNGD.
steps.searchable_summary: Use ICP-OES to detect Fe leaching and ion content

steps.description: Electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) was applied under different reaction systems.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply EPR under different reaction systems
